"I don't give a rat's ass what the hell you think about me," Rodman told reporters, according to the Associated Press. "I'm saying he (Kim Jong Un) is my friend. I don't give a shit what you think about him. You understand?" A statement issued by a public relations firm on Rodman's behalf on Thursday said the basketball star had been under stress and drinking before making the comments. "Dennis Rodman sincerely apologizes for his recent comments made in the heat of the moment," the statement said. "At the time, Dennis was obviously upset by recent events, but should not have used the choice of words he did." The statement added that Rodman was "truly sorry" for any hurt he had caused to the Bae family and said he had been speaking out of line with his government's official position. Rodman's visit coincides with a period of heightened tension on the Korean peninsula, following a third nuclear test by the North in February 2013 and recent U.N. sanctions aimed at punishing Pyongyang for that test. The United States has repeatedly said it will not negotiate over its citizens who are detained in North Korea, and wants to keep them out of the political debate between Washington and Pyongyang. In December, North Korea released one American detainee, Merrill Newman, 85, after holding him for more than a month on charges of war crimes. North Korea has been criticized by the United Nations and human rights groups for its record on human rights, including reports of political camps where dissidents are held in harsh conditions. Rodman, who calls Kim a "friend for life," has been criticized for not using his influence with the North Korean leader to help secure the release of American prisoners. President Obama’s recent visit to Saudi Arabia should prompt such a reevaluation of the U.S.-Saudi relationship. The United States no longer needs to coddle the despotic monarchy and should end this alliance of convenience. The informal U.S.-Saudi alliance began during World War II, with President Franklin D. Roosevelt realizing that the likely substantial oil deposits under the sands of the Saudi kingdom could satisfy the increasing American need to import foreign oil. He and the founder of the Saudi dynasty in Arabia, King Abdul Aziz ibn Saud, agreed that the United States would provide security for the monarchy in exchange for oil. Obama’s trip to Saudi Arabia was designed to smooth Saudi ruffled feathers over the U.S.-led agreement among the great powers and Iran, the Saudi’s regional archrival, which froze Iran’s nuclear program for ten to fifteen years. Started in Denmark with the development of a way to extract insulin from the pancreas and eventually enzymes, it acquired EMD Crop BioScience in 2010. Novozymes BioAg group now claims more than 50 products in biofertility, biocontrol and bioyield enhancers. Acquisitions continued with the purchases of Natural Industries in 2012 and TJ Technology this past summer. Natural Industries strengthened the group’s portfolio in specialty crops, but also has biofungicide and bioinsecticide products with potential in broad-acre markets. TJ Technology added to the group’s bioyield enhancement product lineup with QuickRoots for a range of broad-acre crops including corn, wheat and soybeans. Charlie Hampton, North American marketing manager, Novozymes BioAg, said one of the benefits of such acquisitions is the investment needed to bring new products to the market can limit smaller companies’ potential. Hampton also believes broad-acre growers are becoming more aware of plant development and the role that biologicals can play in it. “They aren’t looking for 20- to 30-bushel yield bumps, but rather consistent yield increases,” he said. “If we can produce three- to five-bushel increases consistently in soybeans and five to eight bushels in corn, growers will stay with us. texts.full_text: their asthmatic condition and treatment which are rarely recognized or addressed in regular clinical consultations. After reviewing the literature in this field, we have identified that improving patient knowledge can be effective in augmenting engagement, and considerably improving their clinical outcomes. It is necessary to develop an effective educational intervention that can help Asthma patients change their perception about self-efficacy and ultimately reduce the total health care costs incurred. Hence, a sound transfer of knowledge during the transition from childcare to adult care is highly recommended. On these very lines, Georgia Institute of Technology designed an interactive educational application called Asthma Academy in conjunction with Children's Healthcare of Atlanta. This website resides in the public cloud and uses a novel animation video-based curriculum to deliver essential healthcare education to asthmatic adolescents in an interactive manner. What distinguAsthma Academy: What Distinguishes It? The Asthma Academy stands out through several innovative features and strategic approaches that differentiate it from traditional asthma education models: 1. Interactive, Animation-Based Curriculum The platform leverages engaging animated videos tailored to adolescents, making complex medical concepts accessible and relatable. This visual and interactive approach caters to teens’ learning preferences, fostering better retention and engagement compared to static educational materials. 2. Cloud-Based Accessibility Hosted on the public cloud, the application ensures seamless access from any device, enabling consistent education delivery regardless of geographic location. This scalability supports widespread adoption and long-term patient engagement. 3. Focus on Adolescent Misconceptions By addressing common myths and gaps in knowledge (e.g., medication adherence, trigger identification), the program directly targets barriers to effective self-management, which are often overlooked in clinical settings. 4. Transition-Focused Design It bridges the gap between pediatric and adult healthcare by equipping teens with the skills and confidence (self-efficacy) to navigate adult care systems, a critical yet under-addressed challenge in asthma management. 5. texts.full_text: double-strand breaks sites. Phosphorylated H2AX (gamma-H2AX) is essential to the efficient recognition and (or) repair of DNA strand breaks (DSBs), and many molecules, often thousands, of H2AX become rapidly phosphorylated at the site of each nascent DSB. An antibody to gamma-H2AX reveals that this highly amplified process generates nuclear foci. The phosphorylation site is a serine four residues from the C-terminus which has been evolutionarily conserved in organisms from giardia intestinalis to humans. Mice and yeast lacking the conserved serine residue or with mutations preventing its phosphorylation exhibit increased sensitivity to ionizing radiation, highlighting the importance of gamma-H2AX for DSB repair mechanisms. texts.full_text: - how to inherit class function? I'm new with python and having some trouble regarding inheritance. i have this class: class A(object): def __init__(self, foo, bar): pass def func1(self): op1() op2() def func2(self): pass I'm using another class that is overriding it : class B(A): def func2(self): # do something the problem is that I want to get with all its operations and add for example op3() so that when using this class I will have the option to use op1, op2 or op3. I've tried using: def func1(self): op3() but it just gave me the option of op3 without op1 and op2. I saw that there is "super" option but I'm not sure I understand how to use it. A: You can call the super-class implementation in func1: class B(A): def func1(self): super(B, self).func1() op3() If you need to call the super-class implementation from func2, then it can be done similarly: class B(A): def func2(self): super(B, self).func2() # do something If you want to call the super-class implementation from func1 in func2, you can do this: class B(A): def func2(self): super(B, self).func2() self.func1() # do something You can also call the super-class implementation in func1 from func2: class B(A): def func2(self): super(B, self).func2() self.func1() # do something ## Appendix B - Parameters Python allows the user to set the parameters for a function in the following manner: def function(arg1, arg2, ...): # function body A function call can be made with the following syntax: function(arg1, arg2, ...) The number of parameters is specified by the number of commas in the function call. In the case of a single parameter, the function call is a single comma. For example, if we want to define a function called "sum" that takes two arguments, we can do it in the following manner: def sum(a, b): return a + b A function call can be made in the following manner: sum(2, 3) In this case, the function call is a single comma. texts.full_text: form_remote_tag, Rails is supposed to handle template rendering automatically and it usually does a good job. However, in some cases it might encounter a discrepancy or inconsistency regarding view paths that needs clarifying. In regards to the error you're experiencing: `Template is missing. Missing template ym4r/gm_plugin/variables/_variable.erb in view path app/views`, one possible explanation could be that Rails is looking for a template under the `ym4r/gm_plugin` directory (presuming YM4R/GM uses that structure) and expecting to find `variables/_variable.erb` in there when, in fact, it should be looking at `app/views`. As for why the issue only surfaces when using form_remote_tag, it may have something to do with this discrepancy in how Rails looks for views. To resolve this, you might try adjusting your view path in the `config/environments/development.rb` file (adjusting to production if needed) to reflect what's actually happening: ```ruby config.action_view.default_url_root = '' config.action_view.root = 'app/views' config.action_view.paths += ['lib/views'] ``` Ensure all your view templates are under `app/views`. Rails does indeed have some strange quirks regarding this so this check is crucial. Another idea is that perhaps you have nested routes and Rails is looking at a path outside of where it actually should be. So in that case, double-check your `config/routes.rb` file to ensure there aren't any misconfigurations with respect to routing paths. Finally, the change you mentioned making to form_remote_tag might still be carrying over some residual settings - maybe an old method name or a different expectation Rails has based on previous configurations - even if you "think" you changed everything back. Rails is very detail-oriented and could catch something subtle like this discrepancy. Lastly, ensure that your `form.remote` helper in your views calls the right JavaScript file and doesn't try to submit to incorrect URLs, which would cause confusion for Rails, leading to an unpredictable rendering process, or in this case, an exception. Also, make sure the JavaScript URL you're specifying can properly resolve the YM4R/GM map into a viewable template. If none of these resolve your particular issue, then there could be another more fundamental problem, such as something with your plugins/extensions,

texts.full_text: a prospective design all reactive arthritis diagnosed at the Service of Rheumatology in Bellvitge Hospital during a thirty months period, from August 1985 to February 1988; in order to describe its clinical features, etiological factors and prognosis. Thirty five patients fulfilled the inclusion criteria for reactive arthritis according to the diagnostic criteria defined by the modified New York classification. The mean age of our patient cohort was 45 years (range: 21-67), with a male predominance (M:F ratio = 3:2). A variety of microbial etiologies were identified; the most common being Chlamydia trachomatis (n=8), followed by Salmonella species (n=5) and Campylobacter jejuni (n=4). The clinical manifestations varied widely among patients but commonly included polyarthritis involving large joints such as the knee or hip, enthesitis, urethritis, and conjunctivitis. The duration of symptoms prior to diagnosis ranged from one month to over five years, highlighting a significant variability in disease onset patterns. texts.full_text: are what a sampling of homeopaths have to say on homeopathy in the care of appendicitis. [Note that the added emphasis is mine] Belladonna and Bryonia are classic homeopathic remedies often used for an inflamed appendix. Using the appropriate homeopathic remedy along with conventional Western medicine may relieve your symptoms and help clear up appendicitis more quickly. However, no scientific literature supports the use of homeopathy for appendicitis. An experienced homeopath would consider your individual case and may recommend treatments to address both your underlying condition and any current symptoms. This statement is repeated at a number of hospital and sites. I cannot tell who originated it; the original citation is to "Integrative Medicine", which may license their content to medical facility sites. At any rate, I have not been able to find out what treatments are used and for what ends Homeopathy Homeopathic treatment for appendicitis is directed at supporting recovery from surgery. It is not meant to be a substitute for surgical treatment. If you suspect appendicitis, seek professional medical treatment for your child immediately. Adam Perlman, a medical doctor who is director of integrative medicine at St. Barnabas Ambulatory Care Center in Livingston, N.J. .says "There is anecdotal evidence that [homeopathy] works, and I won't argue with that," says Perlman, "But if you try to hold homeopathy to the same standards that we hold the rest of Western biomedicine, the evidence isn't there yet." While homeopathy may work for patients seeking holistic, preventive care, Perlman strongly advises against using it for major emergencies like appendicitis. Emergencies like acute abdominal complaints, i.e., acute appendicitis, twisted bowel, fractures and accidents, should first be treated surgically, as should congenital anomalies like harelip, or clubfoot. texts.full_text: Yerevan. It took an hour and the half to get there. That was the autumn of 1993. The winter was dead period, because of no electricity, no transportation and a bunch of snow. The spring '94 came with an excellent offer. The other armenian band Dumbarton Oaks had a rehearsal room in Yerevan, but without recording equipment. We have solved the problem with the equipment (many thanks go to Helen), and started to use their room together. After two months our first demo - Slay the close of yours - was recorded. It took12 hours to finish with that. There were only two recording studios in Yerevan that time, and we decided to choose "Ardzagank". Some of head staff was famous to us in "Ardzagank". Though, the other studio - "Asparez" had better equipment. The demo consist of three songs - Slay the close of yours, Psychosis/Paroxysm and With God's help. Our friend- Vgo helped us with the lyrics for the last one. After that we were invited to the "Barev" TV program. Some pictures you can find on the pictures page. It was our first appearance on the TV. Rumors were saying (after that TV program) that the demo "Slay the close of yours" was not made by Beerdigungs Lauten. It's some other unknown band, who's recordings BL staff pretends to be as their own. Later, those rumors have disappeared after some live shows. texts.full_text: l Scholes, Phil and Gary Neville at Manchester United, will debut in selected across the UK from Sunday December 1 and will be available on DVD from Monday December 2. The film covers the period from 1992, when Giggs first appeared in United's senior team, to 1999, culminating in the club's Champions League triumph. The film tells the story of how six 14-year-old working class lads from diverse backgrounds came together to play for the same club and became the spine of the most lauded team in world football, all the while remaining close friends. The production secured access to all six players and has interviews with, among ot...others, Eric Harrison, the former Manchester United youth team coach, and Alex Ferguson, the former Manchester United manager, to provide a unique and intimate insight into the players' lives and the team's success. The documentary, directed by Benjamin Turner and Gabe Turner, promises to be a must-see for Manchester United and football fans, offering a behind-the-scenes look at the players' journey to the top and the bond that has endured long after their playing careers. The Class of 92 has already generated a lot of buzz, with fans and critics alike eagerly anticipating the release of the film, which is set to be a nostalgic and inspiring look at the careers of some of the most iconic players of their generation. texts.full_text: Karl: Fashion Isn’t To Blame For Galliano’s Meltdown “Who will replace John Galliano?” isn’t the only question fashion industry minders and commentators have been trying to answer since the designer was fired from Dior for making drunken, anti-Semitic statements in public. They’ve also been wondering what led Galliano to drink enough to think those statements were OK to make, and whether the fashion industry is responsible for his vices and those of other high-profile designers. There are worries that the fashion industry causes its talent to burn out, that the pressure to constantly produce and the high-stakes, high-stress environment of fashion design can drive creatives to substance abuse and other self-destructive behaviors. But, as designers Marc Jacobs and Karl Lagerfeld have both suggested, the fashion industry itself isn't To blame for Galliano's meltdown. Jacobs, for one, has spoken about the need for designers to take responsibility for their own actions and to prioritize their own well-being, rather than relying on the industry to provide support. Lagerfeld, on the other hand, has pointed to Galliano's own personal demons, suggesting that the designer's problems run deeper than the stresses of the fashion industry. texts.full_text: a plate of small fried circles that looked exactly like calamari was passed around the table. Ah, finally something I could eat. I popped one in my mouth and began to chew. And chew. And chew. The taste wasn’t bad, but it was a bit like gnawing on a rubber doughnut. I asked my host, an affable young importer of scientific testing equipment for the food industry named Stephen, what it was. “That last part of pig intestine,” he said flatly. “How you say in English . . . asshole?” The joke among Chinese who live in other parts of China is that the Cantonese will eat anything that flies except a plucked chicken and walks on both legs.—Wang Wei’an During my two-week visit to Guangzhou, most things appeared completely different from how I had pictured them. But this was an interesting way it wasn’t. Despite widespread perceptions in much—or at least English-speaking—Western culture—and despite China’s vast differences regionally—I arrived convinced that the people here (whom a man we befriended called Gu’angdong hu, meaning “Cantonese”—not quite Cantonians) were largely Westernized. I had not been completely wrong. On Guangzhou’s packed subway and sidewalks one saw sleekly dressed businesspeople on iPhones working side-by-side with scruffy Chinese youth of all strata smoking clove cigarettes (a trend that has seen something like twenty or more percent growth in recent months), talking animatedly into Nokia phones, their hair greased down over black T-shirts. In my cab ride from the airport to our hotel I sat listening agog as Stephen told me how much money he and his brother have made developing small chemical companies with foreign partners. He was dressed professionally: button-down shirt for most of what looked like a workday, though in another way completely irregular—Friday or Saturday afternoon; slacks that crept up the calf slightly (I asked if this look would cause problems where I grew up—an accident-plagued East Texas town called Lufkin. He said it didn’t matter since no one knew

texts.full_text: ted an Escherichia coli strain to use salvage pathway forthe direct incorporation of exogenous galactose into globotriose using a designer biocatalyst system. This was achieved by overexpressing key enzymes involved in the UDP-galactose salvage pathway alongside Leloir family galactosyltransferases, which are responsible for transferring galactose from UDP-galactose to acceptor substrates. The engineered E. coli strain was designed to efficiently convert exogenous galactose into UDP-galactose through the action of galactokinase (GalK) and galactose-1-phosphate uridylyltransferase (GalT). These enzymes catalyze the phosphorylation of galactose followed by its conversion to UDP-galactose. The subsequent step involved the use of a specific Leloir family β(1,4)-galactosyltransferase that transfers the galactose moiety from UDP-galactose onto globotriose (Gb3), extending it into more complex oligosaccharides. THis mill, one of only two remaining water powered grist mills in the state of Oregon, is powered by the Calapooia. Weathered Textures With paint peeling and its worn appearance, this door has seen a lot in its life at the water powered Thompson's Flour Mill in Shedd, Oregon. Cavitt Creek Bridge The Cavitt Creek bridge can be found just a few miles south of Glide, Oregon on Little River Road. Built in 1943, the bridge saw heavy use from log trucks. From earlier photos, it looks to have sported a peaked archway at both portals, but this appears to have been changed. Central Park fountain Connections The Hawthorne bridge crosses the Willamette River and connects Portland's east and west sides. It is also the oldest operational vertical lift span bridge in the United States. The road crossing the bridge is called Hawthorne Blvd of course, but did you know that Hawthorne is named for Dr. J.C. Hawthorne, who was also founder of Oretha Power & Water Company (owned by Portland General Electric)? Necessary daycare supplies range from outdooR playground equipment and an assortment of toys to high chairs, booster seats, cribs, extra sippy cups and plastic flatware. If you have completed the licensing process you will have already obtained a list of these items. Ensure that all equipment meets current safety standards, and no toys have been recalled. It also is a good idea to stock up on basic art supplies, such as crayons, watercolors, child safety scissors and coloring books. Don't forget your first aid supplies! You are going to want to have on hand a full first aid kit, with plenty of disinfectant, band-aids, antibiotic ointment, burn spray, calamine lotion, sunblock and lotion (for the eventuality the sunblock does not work). It is often beneficial to have Children's Ibuprofen and Tylenol on hand, but bear in mind this should not be administered without a parent's approval. You can choose to advertise your new business by word of mouth, classified ads, fliers-the possibilities are endless. Be sure to have an interview sheet made up when meeting a new child. texts.full_text: o each other by rigid metal rods in order to stabilize the spine and enable progressive bony fusion. Such bone screw-rod constructs have gained prominence due to their superior biomechanical stability relative to alternate fixation techniques, such as wiring, etc., as well as the benefits provided by three column fixation of the spine. Such systems can provide rigid stabilization across multiple spinal segments and facilitate the healing process in conditions like spondylolisthesis, scoliosis, or vertebral fractures. However, traditional screw-rod constructs have limitations when it comes to addressing dynamic stability and accommodating physiological motion post-surgery. The rigidity of these constructs may lead to stress shielding—where adjacent levels of the spine bear increased loads due to reduced load sharing at the fused segment—and can contribute to accelerated degeneration of those segments (adjacent segment disease, ASD). Additionally, in certain cases where there is a need for both compression and distraction within the spinal column, traditional rigid constructs may not offer sufficient flexibility or control over these forces. To address some of these limitations, various innovations have been proposed. For example, dynamic stabilization devices aim to provide more physiological load sharing while still offering enough stability to promote healing. However, many of these solutions are complex in design and require additional surgical steps for implantation, which can increase the risk of complications and prolong recovery times. The present technology aims to improve upon existing spinal fixation systems by providing a compression-distraction mechanism that is integrated into screw-rod constructs. This innovation allows surgeons greater control over the mechanical environment at the fusion site, enabling precise adjustments in both compressive and distractive forces as needed during surgery or post-operatively.
